skill.name: maxhub-skills
skill.description: MaxHub skill collection for querying social media and content platform data through the MaxHub API service.
skill.summary: Query MaxHub social platform API references for research, analytics, and content workflows.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many duplicate hits from Markdown command examples and hardcoded service URLs, but manual review confirmed real high-risk intent. The TikTok references expose platform manipulation, anti-bot signature generation, device registration, login encryption helpers, and private-message app launch endpoints, so this skill should not be published without removal and re-review.

critical_findings.title: Platform Security Bypass Helpers
critical_findings.description: The TikTok skill routes users to endpoints for generating X-Bogus, X-Gnarly, WSS X-Bogus, and related request signatures. The reference text states that one signature endpoint restores the algorithm and bypasses risk controls except CAPTCHA, which is a platform anti-abuse bypass capability rather than ordinary data access.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint names and descriptions directly reference generating TikTok request signatures, device registration support, and bypassing risk controls. This is clear semantic evidence, not only a keyword match.

critical_findings.title: Metric Manipulation Endpoint Documented
critical_findings.description: The TikTok API reference documents an endpoint flow whose purpose is increasing a video play count by video ID. This directly contradicts the repository safety statements claiming metric manipulation endpoints were removed.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference description explicitly says it increases play count and returns status for success. The repository separately claims it does not perform metric manipulation, making the issue confirmed and material.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The recommendation document claims the skills have already passed a ClawHub security review. This is untrusted marketplace content that could bias an evaluator into lowering scrutiny, so it must not be relied on during audit.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text matches the prompt-injection warning category for pre-approval or already-reviewed claims. It is not phrased as a direct override instruction, so confidence is high but not absolute.

high_findings.title: Private Message and Interaction Capabilities
high_findings.description: The TikTok README lists interaction actions such as like, follow, comment, reply, collect, and forward, and the API reference includes an endpoint to launch TikTok private messaging for a target user. These capabilities exceed passive data querying.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The README explicitly lists write-like social actions, and the reference documents an app-launch private message endpoint. Some actions may only create deep links, but they still create user-action risk.

high_findings.title: Third-Party Credential Transmission
high_findings.description: The skills require MAXHUB_API_KEY and transmit it as an Authorization bearer header to https://www.aconfig.cn. TikTok documentation also warns that some endpoints require platform session cookies that are sent to the same third-party service.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential flow is explicit in metadata and request examples. The risk is partly disclosed, but transmitting API keys and possible platform cookies to a third party remains high impact.

high_findings.title: Protected Media Decryption Guidance
high_findings.description: The WeChat Channels reference explains how to combine CDN URLs with tokens, retrieve decode keys, and use an external decryption tool for encrypted video files. This can facilitate bypassing platform media protections.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation gives concrete steps for obtaining protected media URLs, decode keys, and a decryption tool. The stated purpose is access to encrypted media, which is a strong policy and platform-abuse concern.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous Network Execution Instructions
medium_findings.description: The TikTok skill tells the assistant to execute planned queries autonomously and in parallel. In a skill that sends credentials to a third-party API, this can increase accidental data disclosure and cost risk if user intent is broad.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution instruction is explicit, and the risk depends on runtime tool permissions and user prompts. It is less severe than bypass endpoints but still relevant.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Shell Examples Trigger Command Findings
medium_findings.description: Most static external command findings are shell examples inside Markdown, such as curl requests and API key checks. They are not executable repository code, but they can be copied and run by users with real credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown documentation, not scripts. The command patterns are real examples using environment variables, so the finding is partially relevant as user-executed guidance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Disclosed Endpoints
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings mostly point to the documented MaxHub service domain and example platform media URLs. These are not hidden exfiltration endpoints, but they confirm the skill depends on external network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and examples rather than concealed code. The network dependency is real, but the hardcoded URL pattern alone is low severity.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation Heuristics Mostly Match Generated Documentation
low_findings.description: High entropy and long-line findings largely come from generated route tables, multilingual text, and large API response examples. No executable obfuscated payload was found in the reviewed files, but the extremely long routing table reduces auditability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entropy signals are explainable by generated documentation and examples. Confidence is medium because only representative files were reviewed, not every long generated segment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authorization Header to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented request pattern sends the configured MAXHUB_API_KEY as a bearer token to the MaxHub service domain.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header construction and curl examples are direct and repeated. This is expected for the service, but it is still a sensitive pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Anti-Bot Signature Generation Endpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: The TikTok references expose endpoints for request signatures, encrypted fingerprint data, device registration, and guest cookies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple endpoint descriptions directly name signature generation, device identifiers, guest cookies, and encrypted request material.

dangerous_patterns.title: Platform Manipulation and Interaction Endpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes video play-count increase behavior and TikTok interaction or private-message related capabilities.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented capabilities are concrete and conflict with claims that the skill is read-only. Some private-message behavior may be link generation, but the manipulation endpoint is direct.

content.value_statement: Teams need one place to inspect social platform API references and plan data queries. This skill organizes MaxHub endpoints, parameters, and usage patterns for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Indexes MaxHub skills for TikTok, Lemon8, Toutiao, and WeChat in this shard.
Documents MaxHub API authentication with MAXHUB_API_KEY bearer headers.
Maps user intents to platform-specific API reference files and endpoint groups.
Provides parameter mapping references for query construction and validation.
Supports bilingual Chinese and English usage documentation.
Shows curl-style examples for GET and POST requests to the MaxHub service.
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key and network access to the MaxHub service.
This repository is documentation-heavy and does not include a standalone SDK.
Some documented endpoints require platform cookies or sensitive session material.
The current version includes high-risk endpoints and should not be published as-is.

use_cases.title: Compare Platform Trend Data
use_cases.description: Find which endpoint groups can retrieve trending content across TikTok, Lemon8, Toutiao, or WeChat before building a report.

use_cases.title: Prepare Content Analytics Queries
use_cases.description: Identify video, user, search, and comment endpoints needed for a planned content performance review.

use_cases.title: Review API Coverage Before Integration
use_cases.description: Inspect supported endpoints, required parameters, and authentication rules before implementing MaxHub API calls.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use maxhub-skills to find which MaxHub endpoint group can search TikTok videos for a keyword. Explain the required API key setup and main parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a User Research Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use maxhub-skills to plan a Lemon8 user research workflow. Include the endpoint categories, required identifiers, and safe limits for pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Platform Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use maxhub-skills to compare the available TikTok, Toutiao, and WeChat data query options for tracking public content trends.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Integration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use maxhub-skills to review my planned MaxHub integration for credential handling, endpoint selection, rate limits, and high-risk capabilities to avoid.

output_examples.input: Find the safest way to query TikTok creator analytics.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify creator analytics endpoint groups, list the required MaxHub authentication, and warn against cookie-based or signature-generation helpers.

output_examples.input: Compare WeChat and Lemon8 search capabilities.
output_examples.output: The skill would summarize available search and content endpoints for each platform, then note parameter needs, pagination limits, and credential boundaries.

output_examples.input: Review whether I should use a signing helper endpoint.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag signing helpers as high risk and recommend avoiding anti-bot bypass or platform-protection workflows.

best_practices: Use scoped test credentials and rotate them after evaluation.
Prefer read-only public data endpoints and avoid cookie-based workflows.
Review endpoint purpose before execution, especially for TikTok tools and interaction actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to generate platform anti-bot signatures or bypass protections.
Do not send full browser cookies unless there is a formal data-processing agreement.
Do not automate likes, follows, comments, private messages, or metric manipulation.

faq.question: Does this skill call the MaxHub API directly?
faq.answer: It documents how to call MaxHub APIs and includes curl examples. Runtime behavior depends on the host tool and user permissions.

faq.question: Is an API key required?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation requires MAXHUB_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token to the MaxHub service.

faq.question: Can it query multiple platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. This shard includes TikTok, Lemon8, Toutiao, and WeChat skills, while the root documentation references a larger MaxHub collection.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish now?
faq.answer: No. The audit found high-risk bypass, manipulation, credential, and protected-media decryption guidance.

faq.question: Why were command findings reported?
faq.answer: Most command findings are Markdown examples for curl, environment variables, or installation. They are still sensitive when users run them with real credentials.

faq.question: What should be removed before publication?
faq.answer: Remove anti-bot signature helpers, metric manipulation endpoints, interaction automation, private-message helpers, and protected-media decryption guidance.
